rationale: Clear, well-organized opinion piece with a logical progression from problem to argument to counterargument to solution. The writing is mostly direct, though it leans on some rhetorical flourishes ('the great equalizers,' 'penny wise and pound foolish,' 'turn the page') and occasional inflated phrasing ('the city's most vital community center'). The main weakness is specificity: claims about ROI ('well-documented' but no actual figures cited), the 6 p.m. closing time is asserted without sourcing, and the 'negligible' cost claim is hand-waved. The examples of excluded users (single parent, student, freelancer) are effective but somewhat stock. Voice is engaging and reader-aware, with a strong opening hook and closing line, though it occasionally tips into op-ed boilerplate. Structure is the strongest element—each paragraph has a clear purpose and transitions are smooth. Slight repetition in hammering the equity/access point across multiple paragraphs keeps brevity from a 5.
